mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL indicates accelerating inflation expectations, prompting equity investors to demand higher risk premiums and reducing the present value of future corporate earnings, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of months with rising CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, labor market slack severely constrains consumer spending growth and corporate profit margins, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5d returns in >70% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 9.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in RSAFS and HOUST signals robust household balance sheet repair and housing market recovery, which compounds consumer demand and drives sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive 60d return in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining INDPRO during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, forcing earnings downgrades and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20d returns in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding PAYEMS directly increases aggregate wage income and consumer purchasing power, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions correlate with negative 5d S&P 500 returns in >65% of monthly releases.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, extreme monetary accommodation suppresses bond yields and forces portfolio rebalancing into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 60d returns in >60% of months where FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where GDPC1 growth outpaces CPIAUCSL indicates real economic expansion without inflationary pressure, which improves corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Periods of real GDP outpacing inflation fail to produce positive 20d returns in >55% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO during a high unemployment regime signals early-cycle supply recovery that precedes earnings revisions, triggering rapid positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during high unemployment periods yield negative 5d returns in >65% of cases.
